title: Vision test for driver's licenses
stimulus: The vision test for obtaining a driver’s license should not be limited to measuring the adequacy of vision in daylight conditions, as is the current practice. Many people whose daylight vision is adequate have night vision that is inadequate for safe night driving. Most car accidents occur at night, and inadequate vision plays a role in 80 percent of these accidents.
main_conclusion: The vision test for obtaining a driver’s license should not be limited to measuring the adequacy of vision in daylight conditions, as is the current practice.
why: The author states the recommendation immediately, and the rest of the paragraph supports it.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: Many people whose daylight vision is adequate have night vision that is inadequate for safe night driving.

title: Freedom of speech / Policy Adviser
stimulus: Policy Adviser: Freedom of speech is not only a basic human right; it is also the only rational policy for this government to adopt. When ideas are openly aired, good ideas flourish, silly proposals are easily recognized as such, and dangerous ideas can be responded to by rational argument. Nothing is ever gained by forcing citizens to disseminate their thoughts in secret.
main_conclusion: Freedom of speech is not only a basic human right; it is also the only rational policy for this government to adopt.
why: The author’s policy judgment comes first, and the later claims justify it.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: When ideas are openly aired, good ideas flourish, silly proposals are easily recognized as such, and dangerous ideas can be responded to by rational argument.

title: Trade Europe-East Asia / yeti analogy
stimulus: Arguing that there was no trade between Europe and East Asia in the early Middle Ages because there are no written records of such trade is like arguing that the yeti, an apelike creature supposedly existing in the Himalayas, does not exist because there have been no scientifically confirmed sightings. A verifiable sighting of the yeti would prove that the creature does exist, but the absence of sightings cannot prove that it does not.
main_conclusion: Arguing that there was no trade between Europe and East Asia in the early Middle Ages because there are no written records of such trade is like arguing that the yeti, an apelike creature supposedly existing in the Himalayas, does not exist because there have been no scientifically confirmed sightings.
why: The analogy claim is stated first. The second sentence supports that analogy.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: The absence of sightings cannot prove that it does not.

title: No computer / human minds / mechanical rules
stimulus: No computer will ever be able to do everything that some human minds can do, for there are some problems that cannot be solved by following any set of mechanically applicable rules. Yet computers can only solve problems by following some set of mechanically applicable rules.
main_conclusion: No computer will ever be able to do everything that some human minds can do.
why: The conclusion appears first. The “for” clause supports it, and the final sentence adds more support.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: There are some problems that cannot be solved by following any set of mechanically applicable rules.

title: Toy-labeling law / Consumer advocate
stimulus: Consumer advocate: The toy-labeling law should require manufacturers to provide explicit safety labels on toys to indicate what hazards the toys pose. The only labels currently required by law are labels indicating the age range for which a toy is intended. For instance, a “three and up” label is required on toys that pose a choking hazard for children under three years of age. Although the current toy-labeling law has indeed reduced the incidence of injuries to children from toys, parents could prevent such injuries almost entirely if toy labels provided explicit safety information.
main_conclusion: The toy-labeling law should require manufacturers to provide explicit safety labels on toys to indicate what hazards the toys pose.
why: The recommendation is given right away. The later sentences provide background, example, concession, and support.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Background / Example / Concession / Support
follow_up_answer: Parents could prevent such injuries almost entirely if toy labels provided explicit safety information.

title: Diet refined sugar / adult-onset diabetes
stimulus: It is inaccurate to say that a diet high in refined sugar cannot cause adult-onset diabetes, since a diet high in refined sugar can make a person overweight, and being overweight can predispose a person to adult-onset diabetes.
main_conclusion: It is inaccurate to say that a diet high in refined sugar cannot cause adult-onset diabetes.
why: The main clause is the conclusion. The “since” clause gives the support.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: A diet high in refined sugar can make a person overweight.

title: Legal theorist / incarceration / serious threat
stimulus: Legal theorist: It is unreasonable to incarcerate anyone for any other reason than that he or she is a serious threat to the property or lives of other people. The breaking of a law does not justify incarceration, for lawbreaking proceeds either from ignorance of the law or of the effects of one’s actions, or from the free choice on the part of the lawbreaker. Obviously mere ignorance cannot justify incarcerating a lawbreaker, and even free choice on the part of the lawbreaker fails to justify incarceration, for free choice proceeds from the desires of an agent, and the desires of an agent are products of genetics and environmental conditioning, neither of which is controlled by the agent.
main_conclusion: It is unreasonable to incarcerate anyone for any other reason than that he or she is a serious threat to the property or lives of other people.
why: The broad claim is sentence 1. The later claims build a support chain underneath it, including an intermediate conclusion.
structure_map: Main conclusion -> Intermediate conclusion -> Layered support
follow_up_answer: The breaking of a law does not justify incarceration.

title: Nylon industry spokesperson / cotton vs nylon natural
stimulus: Nylon industry spokesperson: Even though cotton and nylon are used for similar purposes, some people have the mistaken notion that cotton is natural but nylon is not. However, nylon’s main components come from petroleum and from the nitrogen in the atmosphere. Clearly the atmosphere is natural. And petroleum comes from oil, which in turn comes from ancient plants-a natural source.
main_conclusion: Nylon’s main components come from petroleum and from the nitrogen in the atmosphere.
why: The first sentence sets up the mistaken view. “However” introduces the author’s actual position.
structure_map: Opposing view -> Rebuttal conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: Some people have the mistaken notion that cotton is natural but nylon is not.

title: Mayor McKinney / wealthy residents / property taxes
stimulus: Mayor McKinney’s policies have often been criticized on the grounds that they benefit only wealthy city residents, but that is not a fair evaluation. Some of McKinney’s policies have clearly benefited the city’s less affluent residents. McKinney actively supported last year’s proposal to lower the city’s high property taxes. Because of this tax decrease, more development is taking place in the city, helping to end the housing shortage and stabilize the rents in the city.
main_conclusion: That is not a fair evaluation.
why: The criticism comes first, then the author pushes back against it.
structure_map: Opposing viewpoint -> Rebuttal conclusion -> Support -> Example -> Support
follow_up_answer: Mayor McKinney’s policies have often been criticized on the grounds that they benefit only wealthy city residents.

title: Some legislators / public funds for scientific research / molds & antibiotics
stimulus: Some legislators refuse to commit public funds for new scientific research if they cannot be assured that the research will contribute to the public welfare. Such a position ignores the lessons of experience. Many important contributions to the public welfare that resulted from scientific research were never predicted as potential outcomes of that research. Suppose that a scientist in the early twentieth century had applied for public funds to study molds: who would have predicted that such research would lead to the discovery of antibiotics-one of the greatest contributions ever made to the public welfare?
main_conclusion: Such a position ignores the lessons of experience.
why: For this drill, this should be taught as rebuttal-family. The first sentence presents a position, and the second sentence rejects it.
structure_map: Opposing position -> Rebuttal conclusion -> Support -> Illustration
follow_up_answer: Some legislators refuse to commit public funds for new scientific research if they cannot be assured that the research will contribute to the public welfare.

title: Letter to the editor / Judge Mosston
stimulus: Letter to the editor: I was shocked to learn that Judge Mosston was convicted of criminal assault, but I disagree with my fellow citizens who believe that he should be forced to resign. I have played cards with Judge Mosston for many years, and he has always shown himself to be fair toward me and our fellow cardplayers. Our judicial system simply cannot afford to lose any just and fair judges.
main_conclusion: I disagree with my fellow citizens who believe that he should be forced to resign.
why: The first clause is concession/background. The real point comes after “but.”
structure_map: Concession / background -> Rebuttal conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: My fellow citizens believe that he should be forced to resign.

title: Witnessing violence in movies / rhetorical-question rebuttal
stimulus: Some people believe that witnessing violence in movies will discharge aggressive energy. Does watching someone else eat fill one’s own stomach?
main_conclusion: Does watching someone else eat fill one’s own stomach?
why: The second sentence is a rhetorical-question rebuttal of the first sentence’s view.
structure_map: Opposing viewpoint -> Rhetorical rebuttal conclusion
follow_up_answer: Some people believe that witnessing violence in movies will discharge aggressive energy.

title: Computers replacing teachers / understanding vs facts-and-rules
stimulus: Behind the hope that computers can replace teachers is the idea that the student’s understanding of the subject being taught consists in knowing facts and rules, the job of a teacher being to make the facts and rules explicit and convey them to the student, either by practice drills or by coaching. If that were indeed the way the mind works, the teacher could transfer facts and rules to the computer, which would replace the teacher as drillmaster and coach. But since understanding does not consist merely of knowing facts and rules, but of the grasp of the general concepts underlying them, the hope that the computer will eventually replace the teacher is fundamentally misguided.
main_conclusion: The hope that the computer will eventually replace the teacher is fundamentally misguided.
why: The paragraph explains the hope being criticized, then the final sentence rejects it.
structure_map: Opposing setup -> Conditional background -> Support -> Rebuttal conclusion
follow_up_answer: The hope that computers can replace teachers.

title: Golden Lake Development / so-called environmentalists / bird migration
stimulus: So-called environmentalists have argued that the proposed Golden Lake Development would interfere with bird-migration patterns. However, the fact that these same people have raised environmental objections to virtually every development proposal brought before the council in recent years indicates that their expressed concern for bird migration patterns is nothing but a mask for their antidevelopment, antiprogress agenda. Their claim, therefore, should be dismissed without further consideration.
main_conclusion: Their claim should be dismissed without further consideration.
why: The opposing claim is introduced first. The author attacks it, then reaches the final dismissal conclusion.
structure_map: Opposing viewpoint -> Intermediate conclusion -> Final rebuttal conclusion
follow_up_answer: So-called environmentalists have argued that the proposed Golden Lake Development would interfere with bird-migration patterns.

title: Long-term cigarette smoking / tobacco companies / candy-cavities analogy
stimulus: That long-term cigarette smoking can lead to health problems including cancer and lung disease is a scientifically well-established fact. Contrary to what many people seem to believe, however, it is not necessary to deny this fact in order to reject the view that tobacco companies should be held either morally or legally responsible for the poor health of smokers. After all, excessive consumption of candy undeniably leads to such health problems as tooth decay, but no one seriously believes that candy eaters who get cavities should be able to sue candy manufacturers.
main_conclusion: It is not necessary to deny this fact in order to reject the view that tobacco companies should be held either morally or legally responsible for the poor health of smokers.
why: The first sentence is background fact. The conclusion comes as a pushback against what many people believe.
structure_map: Background fact -> Opposing setup -> Rebuttal conclusion -> Analogy support
follow_up_answer: The view that tobacco companies should be held either morally or legally responsible for the poor health of smokers.

title: Pharmacists / doctors selling medicine / monopoly objection
stimulus: Patient: Pharmacists maintain that doctors should not be permitted to sell the medicine that they prescribe because doctors would then be tempted to prescribe unnecessary medicines in order to earn extra income. But pharmacists have a financial interest in having a monopoly on the sale of prescription medicines, so their objection to the sale of medicines by doctors cannot be taken seriously.
main_conclusion: Their objection to the sale of medicines by doctors cannot be taken seriously.
why: The pharmacists’ position is introduced first. The author then pivots and rejects it.
structure_map: Opposing viewpoint -> Support for opposing viewpoint -> Support for author -> Rebuttal conclusion
follow_up_answer: Pharmacists maintain that doctors should not be permitted to sell the medicine that they prescribe.

title: Broadcaster / private lives of local celebrities / public interest
stimulus: Broadcaster: Our radio station has a responsibility to serve the public interest. Hence, when our critics contend that our recent exposé of events in the private lives of local celebrities was excessively intrusive, we can only reply that the overwhelming public interest in these matters makes it our responsibility to publicize them.
main_conclusion: The overwhelming public interest in these matters makes it our responsibility to publicize them.
why: The station’s duty is support/principle. The real conclusion is the reply to the critics.
structure_map: Support / principle -> Opposing viewpoint -> Rebuttal conclusion
follow_up_answer: Our critics contend that our recent exposé of events in the private lives of local celebrities was excessively intrusive.

title: Thomas / Municipal Building fire / must-have-seen-it
stimulus: The fire that destroyed the Municipal Building started before dawn this morning, and the last fire fighters did not leave until late this afternoon. No one could have been anywhere in the vicinity of a fire like that one and fail to notice it. Thomas must have seen it, whatever he now says to the contrary. He admits that, as usual, he went from his apartment to the library this morning, and there is no way for him to get from his apartment to the library without going past the Municipal Building.
main_conclusion: Thomas must have seen it.
why: For this drill, this should be forced into rebuttal-family because the conclusion explicitly pushes against a contrary position.
structure_map: Background -> Support principle -> Rebuttal-flavored conclusion -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: Whatever he now says to the contrary.

title: Figurative painting revival / late 1970s
stimulus: There is no mystery as to why figurative painting revived in the late 1970s. People want to look at recognizable images. Sorting out art theories reflected in abstract paintings is no substitute for the sense of empathy that comes from looking at a realistic painting of a figure in a landscape. Perhaps members of the art-viewing public resented abstract art because they felt that its lack of realistic subject matter was a rejection of the viewers and their world.
main_conclusion: There is no mystery as to why figurative painting revived in the late 1970s.
why: The author’s evaluative point comes first, and the rest explains it.
structure_map: Conclusion -> Support -> Support -> Speculative support
follow_up_answer: People want to look at recognizable images.

title: Multinational grain companies / food-distribution / since-clause leading
stimulus: Since multinational grain companies operate so as to maximize profits, they cannot be relied on to initiate economic changes that would reform the world’s food-distribution system. Although it is true that the actions of multinational companies sometimes do result in such economic change, this result is incidental, arising not from the desire for reform but from the desire to maximize profits. The maximization of profits normally depends on a stable economic environment, one that discourages change.
main_conclusion: They cannot be relied on to initiate economic changes that would reform the world’s food-distribution system.
why: The sentence opens with support, but the main clause is still the conclusion, so this is first- sentence family.
structure_map: Support introduced by since -> Main conclusion -> Concession -> Support -> Support
follow_up_answer: Multinational grain companies operate so as to maximize profits.
